questionHtml: Show that the gravitational potential VP at the surface of P due to the mass of P is given by VP = −gP RP where RP is the radius of the planet.
markschemeHtml:
VP = -GMRP AND  gP = GMRP2 (at surface) ✓

Suitable working and cancellation of G and M seen ✓

VP = −gP RP

Must see negative sign

meta.title: Show that the gravitational potential VP at the surface of P due to the mass of P is given by VP = −gP RP where RP is the radius of the planet.
